free_text: One-neutron knockout from light neutron-rich nuclei at
relativistic energies.
A fully ionized Ar-40 primary beam was accelerated
in the SIS heavy ion synchrotron up to 700 MeV/nucleon
and directed toward a 4 g/cm**2 Be target located at
the entrance of the FRS.
A 1720 mg/cm**2 Be target was used.
The experiment was performed using the
Fragment Separator(FRS).
Inverse kinematics
multiple-sample ionization chambers (MUSIC)
plastic scintillation pairs detectors were
used for determination the mass-to-charge ratio
obtained from a time-of-flight measurement.
The six time-projection chambers (TPC)
No information about source of uncertainty
Table 1 of Phys.Rev.,C82(2010)024305
